finding: `taskflow pre-close` still emitted raw `git restore -- <files>` as the accidental-drift recovery path for dirty in-scope delivery files even after staging-index remediation had moved to ATM leases.
expectedBehavior: No taskflow/operator remediation should recommend raw `git restore`, `git reset`, `git checkout`, or `git clean`; destructive worktree or index mutation must route through a governed ATM lease/audit path.
evidenceOrRepro: Reproduced during TASK-GIT-0015 pre-close: `ATM_TASKFLOW_PRECLOSE_SCOPE_TRACKED_DIRTY` suggested `git restore -- "packages/cli/src/commands/git-governance.ts" ...`. TASK-GIT-0015 replaced that hint with `node atm.mjs git lease destructive-override ...` and removed a raw `git restore` rollback hint from git-admission proposal generation plus release hygiene guidance.
followUp: Keep a regression scanning taskflow/git-governance operator hints for raw destructive Git remediation commands; allow only negative examples such as "do not use".
